what_it_does: Reads a JSON design-token registry (tokens.json), resolves primitive references, emits a CSS :root variable block, optionally appends a verbatim components.css, and writes the result to design-system.css.
observations: The output CSS carries a generated-file warning not to hand-edit it.
It emits @import lines before the :root block.
Token groups can have names and notes, emitted as CSS comments.
Each token is either a direct value ('v') or a reference ('ref') to a primitive.
Missing or unknown primitive references raise loud runtime errors.
Extra root entries are appended verbatim from root_extra.
components.css is appended verbatim if it exists.
standouts: Fail-loud validation of primitive references and token shape.
Single-source-of-truth pattern: edit tokens.json, regenerate CSS.
conventions: Docstring explains usage and file relationships at the top.
Type hints on the public function.
Fail-fast error handling with explicit messages.
Generated artifact carries a do-not-edit header.
concerns: No JSON schema validation; malformed structure causes opaque crashes.
Hardcoded relative paths assume a fixed directory layout.
No atomic write, so a crash can leave a partial design-system.css.
components.css is concatenated without a separator comment.
Duplicate token names across groups would silently overwrite earlier ones.
CSS values are inserted without escaping or validation.
root_extra keys are not validated as CSS custom properties.
notes: A small build-time script that turns a JSON token registry into a CSS variable sheet and merges a companion component stylesheet.
questions: What is the exact schema of tokens.json?
Is components.css hand-written or produced by another tool?
How are token/primitive changes versioned or reviewed?
Are duplicate token names across groups intended to be allowed?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it compiles tokens.json into design-system.css with primitive resolution and appends components.css.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for design tokens
Separation of primitive values and semantic token references
intention_signals: It appears designed to let designers or maintainers edit a JSON token registry and regenerate CSS automatically, using primitive references to keep related tokens consistent.
novelty: The explicit, fail-loud primitive-reference resolution inside an otherwise minimal JSON-to-CSS emitter.
contribution: A deterministic build step that transforms a JSON token registry into a CSS :root variable sheet and merges a hand-written component CSS file.
summary_for_embedding: emit.py is a build script that reads tokens.json, resolves primitive references, emits a CSS :root block, appends components.css if present, and writes design-system.css.
intention_for_embedding: The file was intended to automate generation of a design-system CSS file from a JSON token registry while preserving a hand-written component stylesheet.

symbols.does: Converts a loaded tokens dictionary into the full design-system.css text.
symbols.description: The emit function builds the generated CSS string: it writes a header, emits @import rules, opens a :root block, iterates token groups emitting comments and CSS custom properties, resolves token references against primitives with loud failures, appends root_extra entries, and closes the block.

symbols.does: Loads tokens.json, calls emit, appends components.css if it exists, and writes design-system.css.
symbols.description: The main entry point determines the file's directory, reads tokens.json, invokes emit to produce CSS, reads components.css verbatim when present and concatenates it, then writes the combined output to design-system.css and prints a summary.
